title: Dragon-dmg
description: Leaderboard and rewards for Ender Dragon damage during dragon fights.
body:
✦ **Description**

A damage leaderboard and killer announcement for when the Ender Dragon is killed, with configurable messages. Awards can also be given to players for killing or damaging the dragon.

![Example leaderboard image](https://i.imgur.com/cxq9WKN.png)

✦ **Features**

- Announce who kills the Ender Dragon, toggleable in the config.
- Shows a leaderboard of how much damage every player did to the Ender Dragon.
- Shows crystals destroyed by each player on the leaderboard.
- Configure messages to only show in a specific world.
- Give awards to players for killing the dragon or give a random chance award based on damage done, e.g. 20 damage out of 100 total has a 20% chance of giving the reward.


✦ **Permissions**

- dragondmg.dragondmg - Allow running /dragondmg

✦ **Commands**

- /Dragondmg reload - Reload the config (dragondmg.dragondmg)

✦ **Config/Setup**

An up to date config is available [here](https://github.com/hraponssi/Dragon-dmg/blob/main/src/main/resources/config.yml).

Config Values:

- dmglisttitle - The title for the damage leaderboard in chat
- dmglistentry - The format for players listed in the damage leaderboard
- killermsg - The format for announcing the killer of the dragon
- announcekiller - Toggle announcing who killed the dragon
- worldonlymsg - Toggle only sending msgs to the world configured below
- worldname - Name of the world for the above setting
- enablexprewards - Toggle giving XP rewards
- damagexpmultiplier - Toggle multiplying XP by damage percentage or giving a flat rate
- basexp - Base XP amount for XP rewards
- crystalrewardboost - Add extra percentage based on crystals destroyed to the damage percentage when giving rewards.
- crystalboostpercent - Percent to add for the prior setting per crystal destroyed
- enablekillerrewards - Toggle giving killer rewards
- executefriendlyworldnames - Toggle using vanilla friendly world names
- killrewards - A list of rewards if the above setting is enabled
- enabledmgrewards - Toggle giving damage rewards
- dmgrewards - A list of rewards if the above setting is enabled

Note: An example execute command is included for killrewards which drops an elytra at the killed dragons location. For the execute command to work with the main overworld, nether, or end "executefriendlyworldnames" must be enabled, as vanilla names differ from spigot ("the_end" vs "world_the_end").

✦ **Issues/Problems**

Please report any issues or problems on the github issue tracker or on the discussions page.

✦ **Links**

https://github.com/hraponssi/Dragon-dmg

https://bstats.org/plugin/bukkit/Dragon-dmg/13878
